_comment: Google Chrome Enterprise Policy for Air-Gapped Extension Deployment
This policy force-installs extensions from local CRX files without
requiring internet access. Deploy this file to:
Windows: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome  (via registry or GPO)
Linux:   /etc/opt/chrome/policies/managed/chrome_policy.json
macOS:   /Library/Managed Preferences/com.google.Chrome.plist
CRX files must be pre-staged at the paths listed below.
Update extension IDs and CRX filenames to match your builds.

_extension_ids._comment: Reference: extension IDs used in this policy

_comment: Force-install extensions by ID.
Format: '<extension_id>;<update_url_or_crx_path>'
For offline/air-gapped installs, use a file:// URL pointing to an
update manifest XML or directly to the CRX location.
Windows paths use file:///C:/browser-extensions/chrome/
Linux paths use file:///opt/browser-extensions/chrome/
Adjust paths below to match your deployment target OS.
Allowlist: only these extension IDs may be installed.
Combined with ExtensionInstallBlocklist '*' this creates a
deny-all-except-these policy.
Blocklist: '*' blocks all extensions not explicitly allowed.
This ensures only allowlisted extensions can be installed.
Per-extension settings.
'installation_mode': 'force_installed' ensures the extension is
installed automatically and cannot be removed by the user.
'update_url': points to a local update manifest for offline install.
'override_update_url': true forces Chrome to use our URL, not the
Web Store URL.
'toolbar_pin': 'force_pinned' pins the extension icon to the toolbar.
The wildcard '*' entry disables updates and blocks all other extensions.

*.blocked_install_message: This extension is not approved. Contact IT.
